title: Delicate Floral Korean Fish Poke
description: A light and velvety Korean dish featuring sashimi-cut fish, full fat sour cream, and floral notes, inspired by the Chungcheong region, and kosher-friendly. This dish is perfect for those looking for a delicate and refreshing seafood experience.
instructions: Cut the sashimi-grade fish into small cubes and place them in a large bowl.
In a small bowl, whisk together the full fat sour cream, Gochujang, honey, rice vinegar, and sesame oil until smooth.
Pour the sour cream mixture over the fish and gently fold until the fish is well coated.
Sprinkle the edible flowers, toasted sesame seeds, and thinly sliced green onions over the top of the fish.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Just before serving, give the fish a gentle stir and adjust the seasoning if needed.
Serve the fish poke chilled, garnished with additional edible flowers if desired.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
